---
title: "SO_R005: App YAML Configuration Rule"
subtitle: "Implementation guidelines for YAML configuration files"
chapter: "CH01"
category: "rule"
number: "SO_R005"
date-created: "2025-04-02"
date-modified: "2025-08-17"
author: "Claude"
type: "rule"
law: "Structure & Organization Law Implementation Rules"
article-number: "Article 5"
implements:
  - "SO_P003: App Construction Principles"
derives_from:
  - "CH00.MP001: Primitive Terms and Definitions"
  - "CH00.MP005: Instance vs. Principle"
  - "CH00.MP010: Information Flow Transparency"
  - "CH00.MP011: Sensible Defaults"
related_to:
  - "SO_P004: App Bottom-Up Construction"
  - "CH00.MP006: Data Source Hierarchy"
  - "CH00.MP008: Terminology Axiomatization"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article Overview {#overview}

**Structure & Organization Law Implementation Rules Article 5 (App YAML Configuration Rule)**

This article establishes specific implementation guidelines for using YAML configuration files in applications, enabling declarative app construction and promoting separation of configuration from implementation.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Rule
Application configuration should be externalized in YAML files that define the structure, components, and data sources without requiring code changes. This enables non-developers to modify application behavior and supports a clear separation between configuration (instances) and implementation (principles).
:::

# Section 1: Configuration Location {#location}

## Subsection 1: Directory Structure {#directory}

All YAML configuration files should be stored in the `app_configs` directory at the project root:

```
precision_marketing_app/
└── app_configs/
    ├── customer_dna_app.yaml   # Main app configuration
    ├── component_config.yaml   # Component-specific configuration
    └── data_sources.yaml       # Data source mappings
```

This follows the Instance vs. Principle Meta-Principle by placing instance-specific configurations outside the global_scripts directory.

# Section 2: YAML Structure Guidelines {#structure}

## Subsection 1: Basic Structure {#basic-structure}

Every YAML configuration file should include:

```yaml
# [File Description]
# [Author]
# [Date Created]
title: [Application Title]
version: [Configuration Version]

# Main configuration sections follow...
```

## Subsection 2: Standard Format Patterns {#format-patterns}

The configuration follows six standard patterns for data source and environment specifications.

:::{.callout-note}
## Key Principle
If there is only one data source, the role is unnecessary; if there are multiple sources, they are discriminated by their roles.
:::

### Pattern 1: Simple String Format
```yaml
component_name: dataset_name
```

### Pattern 2: Array Format
```yaml
component_name: 
  - dataset1
  - dataset2
  - dataset3
```

### Pattern 3: Object Format with Roles
```yaml
component_name:
  role1: dataset1
  role2: dataset2
  role3: dataset3
```

### Pattern 4a: Single Data Source with Parameters
```yaml
component_name:
  data_source: dataset_name    # Single data source
  parameters:                  # Component parameters
    param1: value1
    param2: value2
```

### Pattern 4b: Multiple Data Sources with Roles and Parameters
```yaml
component_name:
  role1: dataset1              # Multiple data sources with roles
  role2: dataset2
  parameters:                  # Component parameters
    param1: value1
    param2: value2
```

### Pattern 5: Environment Configuration Pattern
```yaml
environment_name:
  data_source: "path/to/data"  # Data source path
  parameters:                  # Environment parameters
    param1: value1
    param2: value2
```

### Pattern 6: External Parameter Files Pattern
```yaml
component_name:
  data_source: dataset_name    # Data source
  parameters:                  # Component parameters
    # Simple inline parameters
    param1: value1
    param2: value2
    
    # Complex parameters in external file
    external_params:
      file: "path/to/parameters.xlsx"  # External file path
      sheet: "ParameterSheet"          # Sheet name for Excel files
      refresh_on_startup: true         # Whether to reload on app startup
```

# Section 3: Configuration Components {#components}

## Subsection 1: Brand Configuration {#brand}

Brand-specific settings should be defined in the YAML configuration:

```yaml
# Brand-specific settings
brand:
  name: "COMPANY_NAME"
  language: "chinese"  # Language settings for UI and data
  raw_data_folder: "../rawdata_COMPANY_NAME"
```

## Subsection 2: Component Configuration {#component-config}

Components are organized hierarchically by category:

```yaml
components:
  # Section 1 components
  macro:
    # Simple component with single data source
    overview: sales_summary_view
    
    # Component with data source and parameters
    trends:
      data_source: sales_trends
      parameters:
        show_kpi: true
        refresh_interval: 300
  
  # Section 2 components
  micro:
    # Component with multiple data sources
    customer_profile:
      primary: customer_details
      preferences: customer_preferences
      history: customer_history
```

## Subsection 3: External Parameter Files {#external-params}

For complex component parameters, reference external files:

```yaml
components:
  advanced_analytics:
    data_source: analytics_data
    parameters:
      # Simple inline parameters
      show_filters: true
      default_view: "summary"
      
      # Complex parameters in external file
      model_config:
        file: "app_data/parameters/model_parameters.xlsx"
        sheet: "AdvancedAnalytics"
        refresh_on_startup: true
```

# Section 4: Implementation Guidelines {#implementation}

## Subsection 1: YAML Loading {#loading}

Use the standardized `readYamlConfig` utility:

```r
# Load configuration
config <- readYamlConfig("customer_dna_app.yaml")

# Access configuration values with safe fallbacks
app_title <- config$title %||% "Default Title"
theme_settings <- config$theme %||% list(version = 5, bootswatch = "default")
```

## Subsection 2: Configuration Validation {#validation}

Always validate configurations before using them:

```r
validateConfig <- function(config) {
  required_fields <- c("title", "components")
  missing_fields <- required_fields[!required_fields %in% names(config)]
  
  if (length(missing_fields) > 0) {
    warning("Configuration missing required fields: ", 
            paste(missing_fields, collapse = ", "))
    return(FALSE)
  }
  
  return(TRUE)
}
```

## Subsection 3: External Parameter Loading {#param-loading}

Load external parameter files with explicit source logging:

```r
loadComponentParameters <- function(component_config) {
  params <- component_config$parameters %||% list()
  
  for (param_name in names(params)) {
    param_value <- params[[param_name]]
    
    if (is.list(param_value) && !is.null(param_value$file)) {
      log_info(paste("Loading external parameters from", param_value$file))
      
      file_path <- param_value$file
      file_ext <- tools::file_ext(file_path)
      
      external_params <- switch(
        file_ext,
        xlsx = readxl::read_excel(file_path, sheet = param_value$sheet %||% 1),
        csv = readr::read_csv(file_path),
        json = jsonlite::fromJSON(file_path),
        rds = readRDS(file_path),
        yml = , yaml = yaml::read_yaml(file_path),
        {
          warning(paste("Unsupported file extension:", file_ext))
          NULL
        }
      )
      
      if (!is.null(external_params)) {
        params[[param_name]] <- external_params
        attr(params[[param_name]], "source_file") <- param_value$file
        attr(params[[param_name]], "load_time") <- Sys.time()
      }
    }
  }
  
  return(params)
}
```

# Section 5: Structural Rules and Patterns {#rules}

## Subsection 1: Component Structure Rule {#component-rule}

Components can only contain:
- A direct dataset reference (string format)
- An array of dataset references (array format)
- Named dataset references with roles (object format)
- A data_source field with an optional parameters object

## Subsection 2: Role Assignment Rule {#role-rule}

Roles are only used when there are multiple data sources that need to be distinguished by their function. A single data source should never have a role assigned.

## Subsection 3: Parameter Encapsulation Rule {#param-rule}

All configuration parameters must be contained within a dedicated `parameters` object, which is a sibling to data source definitions.

## Subsection 4: Additional Rules {#additional-rules}

1. **Structure Simplification Rule**: Use the simplest form possible
2. **Hierarchy Containment Rule**: Component-specific config stays within component
3. **Consistency Pattern**: Similar components follow similar patterns
4. **Mutually Exclusive Rule**: Data sources and parameters are separate concepts
5. **External Reference Transparency Rule**: Use explicit file paths
6. **Sensible Defaults Rule**: Rely on defaults wherever possible

# Section 6: Complete Configuration Example {#example}

```yaml
# Customer DNA Analysis Dashboard Configuration
title: AI行銷科技平台
theme:
  version: 5
  bootswatch: cosmo
layout: navbar

# Brand-specific settings
brand:
  name: "COMPANY_NAME"
  language: "chinese"
  raw_data_folder: "../rawdata_COMPANY_NAME"

# Components with their data sources
components:
  macro:
    # Simple string format
    overview: sales_summary_view
    
    # Single data source with parameters
    trends:
      data_source: sales_trends
      parameters:
        show_kpi: true
        refresh_interval: 300
  
  micro:
    # Object format with named roles
    customer_profile:
      primary: customer_details
      preferences: customer_preferences
      history: customer_history
  
  analytics:
    # External parameter files pattern
    predictive_models:
      data_source: customer_data
      parameters:
        prediction_horizon: 90
        confidence_threshold: 0.75
        model_configurations:
          file: "app_data/parameters/model_config.xlsx"
          sheet: "PredictiveModels"
          refresh_on_startup: true

# Environment-specific configurations
environments:
  development:
    data_source: "development_data/"
    parameters:
      debug: true
  production:
    data_source: "app_data/"
    parameters:
      debug: false
```

# Section 7: Benefits {#benefits}

1. **Separation of Concerns**: Configurations separate from implementation
2. **Non-Developer Access**: Non-developers can modify app behavior
3. **Environment Flexibility**: Different configurations for environments
4. **Reduced Code Changes**: App modifications without code changes
5. **Self-Documentation**: YAML format with comments provides documentation
6. **Consistency**: Standardized configuration format across applications

# Implementation Checklist {#checklist}

:::{.callout-note}
## YAML Configuration Checklist

When implementing YAML configuration:

**Structure:**
- [ ] Configuration in app_configs directory
- [ ] Basic structure with title and version
- [ ] Comments documenting sections

**Data Sources:**
- [ ] Appropriate pattern selected
- [ ] Roles only for multiple sources
- [ ] Parameters in separate object

**Components:**
- [ ] Hierarchical organization
- [ ] Consistent patterns within category
- [ ] External files documented

**Implementation:**
- [ ] Configuration validation implemented
- [ ] Safe fallbacks provided
- [ ] External file loading logged
- [ ] Error handling included

**Best Practices:**
- [ ] Sensible defaults used
- [ ] Platform-neutral paths
- [ ] Version controlled
- [ ] DRY principle followed
:::

# Conclusion {#conclusion}

The App YAML Configuration Rule establishes a comprehensive framework for externalizing application configuration, enabling declarative app construction and supporting clear separation between configuration and implementation. This approach improves maintainability, accessibility, and flexibility while maintaining consistency across applications.

---

*Based on: Structure & Organization Law Implementation Rules Article 5*  
*Implements: [SO_P003 App Construction Principles](../../CH01_structure_organization/principles/SO_P003_app_construction.qmd)*  
*Derives from: [MP001](../../CH00_fundamental_principles/MP001.qmd), [MP005](../../CH00_fundamental_principles/MP005.qmd), [MP010](../../CH00_fundamental_principles/MP010.qmd), [MP011](../../CH00_fundamental_principles/MP011.qmd)*
